criteria.label: Define k-fold and stratified k-fold CV and state when to use each.
criteria.example: Write two sentences: k-fold rotates k disjoint validation folds; stratified k-fold preserves class ratios in each fold—use stratified for imbalanced classification.

criteria.label: Run 5-fold CV with a Pipeline and report mean and std of RMSE.
criteria.example: On diabetes data, Pipeline(StandardScaler → Ridge(α=1)), KFold(5), print per-fold RMSE, mean, and std using cross_val_score with neg_root_mean_squared_error.

criteria.label: Explain why preprocessing must be inside each CV fold.
criteria.example: State that fitting a scaler on all X leaks validation statistics; show the wrong pattern (fit on X then CV) vs Pipeline(correct).

criteria.label: Compare two ridge α values using CV and pick a winner without touching the test set.
criteria.example: CV mean RMSE: α=0.1 → 54.2, α=10 → 52.8; choose α=10 on train+val CV, refit, evaluate once on locked test.

criteria.label: Describe nested CV and distinguish evaluation protocol from mitigation use cases.
criteria.example: Sketch outer 5-fold scoring vs inner 3-fold tuning; note that mitigation-focused nested workflows are covered under overfitting-mitigation--cross-validation.
